# Data Fabric for Security | Zscaler

**Source:** https://www.zscaler.com/products-and-solutions/data-fabric
**Captured:** 2026-04-24 via WebFetch.

---

## Primary Heading
"Achieve unparalleled risk insights with the power of all of your security data—plus your unique business logic and processes."

## Core Problem Statement
The page identifies a critical challenge: security leaders manage dozens of siloed tools, each providing isolated data in different formats. This fragmentation makes it nearly impossible to achieve holistic risk understanding across an organization.

## Solution Overview
The Data Fabric aggregates and unifies security data across all tools and business systems, serving as the foundation for Zscaler's Exposure Management portfolio. It enables "feedback loops between products to drive even richer insights."

## Key Capabilities

**Data Ingestion**: Supports multiple formats (JSON, JSONL, CSV, ZIP, XML, ZST, ZSTD) and features "150 pre-built inbound and outbound integrations."

**Harmonization**: Normalizes entities with different names across sources and maps data to a standardized entity model.

**Deduplication**: Identifies and consolidates duplicate findings from multiple tools reporting on the same assets.

**Correlation & Enrichment**: Recognizes relationships between tools and enriches collective understanding of entities.

## Primary Benefits

- Connect all security data sources seamlessly
- Apply custom business logic and scoring
- Create dynamic dashboards for relevant metrics
- Leverage feedback loops with Zscaler products

## Target Use Cases

1. Building CTEM (Continuous Threat Exposure Management) foundations
2. Creating comprehensive asset inventories
3. Risk-based vulnerability prioritization

## Scale
The platform supports 150+ connectors with an "AnySource™ Connector" for custom data sources.

## Context
The Data Fabric is powered by Avalor technology (Zscaler acquisition) and forms the data aggregation backbone for the Exposure Management portfolio, which includes EASM, Risk360, and Unified Vulnerability Management.
